headline: Oil Steady on Signs US, Iran Working Toward Ceasefire Extension
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Oil steadied on signs the US and Iran may extend a ceasefire and restart talks to end the war that’s rocked energy markets.Brent was stable below $95 a barrel after ending Wednesday barely changed, while West Texas Intermediate was near $91. Washington and Tehran are considering a two-week ceasefire extension to allow more time to negotiate a peace deal, according to a person familiar with the matter.In the Strait of Hormuz — which connects the Persian Gulf to global markets — mov

headline: Oil Rises With Hormuz Still Blocked as Longer Truce Under Review
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Oil edged higher as traders weighed signs the US and Iran may extend a ceasefire and restart talks to end the war against a double blockade of the Strait of Hormuz that continues to impede flows.Brent edged above $96 a barrel after ending Wednesday barely changed, while West Texas Intermediate was near $92. Washington and Tehran are considering a two-week ceasefire extension to allow more time to negotiate a peace deal, according to a person familiar with the matter.In the Strait

headline: Trump Officials Urge Oil Industry to Boost Output Amid War
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Trump administration officials urged US oil producers to boost output, driving home a message to the industry that’s become more urgent amid the war in Iran. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and Energy Secretary Chris Wright made their plea during a videoconference Thursday with roughly a dozen oil executives, including representatives of Exxon Mobil Corp., Chevron Corp. and Continental Resources Inc.
